answer:
Twenty supports three primary authentication methods for its API. API keys are best for internal scripts and server-to-server integrations, created via Settings in the Twenty workspace [1][2]. OAuth 2.0 is used for external apps acting on behalf of users, supporting authorization code flow with PKCE or client credentials for server-to-server access [2]. Additionally, JWT tokens are available for session-based web application authentication [1].

For API keys, include the key in the request header [3][4]:

```http
Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY
```

For OAuth, apps must be registered via the dynamic registration endpoint [2]. Once authorized, use the returned access token in the same `Authorization: Bearer` header format for your requests [1][2]. For GraphQL, this header is passed within the request configuration, such as the HttpLink for Apollo or connection params for WebSockets [5].

results.title: Import Data via API - Twenty Documentation
